name: Chocolate Chip Cookie Wreath
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 375°F. Line four cookie sheets with foil and lightly grease.
In a large bowl, beat brown sugar, vegetable shortening, milk, and vanilla extract until well blended. Beat in the egg. In a medium bowl, combine flour, salt, and baking soda. Stir into the mixture just until combined. Add chocolate chips and pecans; divide dough into four portions. Divide each portion into 12 pieces. Roll each piece into a 1-inch ball.
Arrange balls on cookie sheets to form an 8-inch circle, touching slightly. Flatten with fingers. Repeat with remaining balls for three more wreaths. Bake one sheet at a time for 12-14 minutes or until lightly browned. Do not overbake. Cool completely before removing from the sheet.
Meanwhile, prepare icing: Beat powdered sugar, vegetable shortening, milk, and corn syrup in a small bowl until smooth. Adjust consistency with milk or powdered sugar as needed. Cover and refrigerate up to 1 week.
Makes 4 wreaths. Spread or pipe icing over cooled wreaths. Use red and green candies for flowers and leaves; place in clusters around the wreath.
